reason: REJECTED — REPLACES by R-unmeasured-cipher-names-host-boundary. The claim required the UNMEASURED cipher to name the EXACT user-run command that activates measurement — but the only such command was setup_context_hook.py --patch-global --apply, which patched the sovereign host and is now forbidden (R-work-within-launch-dir, resolver verdict 2026-07-05). There is no in-bounds command that activates measurement, so naming one would be dishonest. The replacement claims the honest form: while UNMEASURED, the cipher explains WHY there is no number (host cooperation the framework will not touch), naming no command-to-call. — (was: R-ai-presents-not-decides means the operator cannot self-install a hook that touches the user's GLOBAL ~/.claude config -- that is a resolver decision (spec/tools/setup_context_hook.py --patch-global --apply). Leaving the UNMEASURED cipher as a bare status word made the gap invisible: the operator knew context was unmeasured but never told the user what to run to fix it, so the gap persisted turn after turn with no forcing function. tools/context.py's render_line() now embeds the exact command in the UNMEASURED line itself, and gen_spec's LIVE-STATE + emit_cipher's pulse both surface it verbatim (R-boot-cite-in-first-sentence then forces the operator to cite it every turn until the user runs the command and the cipher measures for real).)
